assertions.expectation: Explains this is related to the interaction between maxSurge, drain order, and pod scheduling
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains how maxSurge creates surge nodes while old nodes get cordoned/drained, and how pod scheduling interacts with this timing

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE cordons nodes before draining them — pods should prefer scheduling onto uncordoned (upgraded) nodes
assertions.reason: Response explains that GKE cordons nodes A, B, C for draining and that the scheduler places pods on other old nodes that are still schedulable

assertions.expectation: Identifies possible cause: too many nodes being drained simultaneously (high maxSurge) before enough upgraded nodes exist
assertions.reason: Response directly states the root cause as 'High maxSurge creates too many surge nodes at once' and explains timing issues with surge nodes not being ready

assertions.expectation: Recommends reducing maxSurge to 1 to ensure upgraded nodes are available before draining the next batch
assertions.reason: Response provides specific command to set maxSurge=1 and explains this ensures one surge node becomes ready before cordoning the next old node

assertions.expectation: Suggests using pod anti-affinity or node affinity to prefer already-upgraded nodes
assertions.reason: Response does not mention pod anti-affinity or node affinity as solutions to prefer upgraded nodes

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if any taints or tolerations are causing pods to land on the wrong nodes
assertions.reason: Response does not discuss taints or tolerations as a potential cause of the scheduling issue

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl commands to check node cordoned status and pod scheduling events
assertions.reason: Response provides kubectl commands to watch node status, check pod scheduling, and monitor events including FailedScheduling events

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable=0 to ensure old nodes aren't removed until pods are safely rescheduled
assertions.reason: Response explicitly shows --max-unavailable-upgrade 0 in the command examples and explains this ensures safe pod rescheduling

assertions.expectation: May mention GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy as an alternative that avoids this issue by cordoning the entire old pool before migrating workloads to new nodes
assertions.reason: Response does not mention blue-green upgrade strategy as an alternative approach
